topic: trying to meal prep with Marco (he can't cook)

turns.content: so Marco and I tried to meal prep together this weekend and it was an absolute disaster lmao
Oh no, what happened? Meal prep with a partner can go either way.

ok so the idea was we'd split the work to make it go faster right?? I was handling the proteins and he was supposed to do the vegetables and the rice. simple enough. except Marco somehow managed to burn rice. RICE. the one thing you literally just boil water for
Burning rice is honestly impressive in a bad way. Did he forget about it on the stove, or was it more of a water ratio issue?

he put it on the stove and then went to watch the game "for just a second" and I could smell it from the other side of the apartment. the pot is ruined. like actually ruined, the bottom is black. and this man looked at me with a straight face and said "I think it's still good if we scrape off the top layer"
The confidence of "scrape off the top layer" when the pot itself is destroyed is truly something. So was the rice a total loss, or did you actually try to salvage any of it?

total loss. I threw the whole pot away I couldn't even look at it anymore. and then I asked him to chop the bell peppers and he was cutting them in these huge uneven chunks like the size of my fist and I was like babe... these are not going to cook evenly... and he got all defensive like "you said chop them you didn't say how small"

The classic "you didn't give me specific enough instructions" defense. To be fair, if someone hasn't cooked much, they genuinely might not know what a properly diced bell pepper looks like. Did you end up just taking over the vegetable situation?

yes and then I felt bad because the whole point was to do it together so he wouldn't feel useless but I also couldn't let him put fist-sized pepper chunks in my meal prep containers I have standards. so I gave him assembly duty which was just putting the food into the containers and labeling them and he was actually good at that lol

Honestly, finding each person's role is the key to cooking together. He's on assembly, portioning, and labeling — that's legitimate work and it keeps things moving. Maybe next time give him the tasks that are harder to mess up: washing vegetables, measuring ingredients, stirring things that are already on the stove. Build up his skills gradually.

that's actually smart start him on the easy stuff and work up. because I love this man but he grew up with his mom and his abuela doing ALL the cooking so he literally never learned. like he can make eggs and that's it. scrambled. not even over easy. and I don't want to be the person who does all the cooking forever you know??

That's completely fair. If you want cooking to be a shared responsibility long-term, the investment in teaching him now pays off later. You could pick one simple recipe a week for him to "own" — like a sheet pan dinner where everything just gets tossed with oil and seasoning and goes in the oven. It's really hard to mess up, and he gets to feel like he made a real meal.

ooh a sheet pan dinner is a good idea because even he can't burn something that's in the oven with a timer on right?? actually don't answer that I don't want to jinx it. should I stand there and supervise or let him figure it out on his own

Let him do it on his own the first time — hover too much and it'll feel like a test instead of him learning. Just be nearby in case he has questions, and resist the urge to jump in and fix things. If the chicken is a little overcooked or the veggies are a little uneven, that's fine. The goal is for him to get comfortable in the kitchen, not for it to be perfect on attempt one.
you're right you're right I need to let go of the control thing. ok I'm going to find a really simple sheet pan recipe and assign it to him as homework. wish me luck lol

Good luck to you and especially to Marco! A simple one to start: chicken thighs, broccoli, and sweet potato, all tossed with olive oil and whatever seasoning you like. Everything at 400 degrees for about 25 minutes. Even if he overcooks it slightly, it's still edible. Baby steps!
